session_handoff.project: Graphify Client Portal — Sub-project 1: Graph Compare Viewer
session_handoff.author: Marcus Daley
session_handoff.purpose: Execution session handoff. Paste into a fresh Claude Code window: 'Read C:/ClaudeSkills/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-03-graph-compare-execution-handoff.json then follow instructions_for_claude exactly.'

instructions_for_claude.step_1: Read this entire JSON before responding.
instructions_for_claude.step_2: Announce: 'Graphify Sub-project 1 — Graph Compare Viewer. Execution session. Dispatching Agent 1 (Scaffold & Shell).'
instructions_for_claude.step_3: Load the plan file at C:/ClaudeSkills/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-03-graph-compare-viewer.md — this contains the full task definitions with exact code for all 6 agents.
instructions_for_claude.step_4: Invoke 'superpowers:subagent-driven-development' skill — this governs how each agent is dispatched and reviewed.
instructions_for_claude.step_5: Dispatch agents SEQUENTIALLY — Task 2 depends on Task 1's output, Task 3 on Task 2, etc. Do NOT dispatch in parallel.
instructions_for_claude.step_6: After each agent completes, do a brief review (check the file was written, no console errors noted), then dispatch the next agent.
instructions_for_claude.step_7: Agent 5 (Extraction Script) can run in parallel with Agents 1-4 if the opportunity arises — it owns only scripts/extract_original_graph.py and .gitignore, no overlap with graph-compare.html.
instructions_for_claude.step_8: Agent 6 (Integration & Validation) runs LAST — after all other agents are done.
instructions_for_claude.step_9: Before each agent writes code, that agent must load 'universal-coding-standards' from C:/ClaudeSkills/skills/universal-coding-standards/.
instructions_for_claude.step_10: Do NOT skip verification steps inside each task. Each agent self-verifies before marking complete.

developer_profile.name: Marcus Daley
developer_profile.background: Navy veteran (9 years leadership), Full Sail BS Online Game Development (Feb 6 2026), stay-at-home dad
developer_profile.focus: Portfolio-grade software — AAA studio practices applied universally. Quality over speed. Never quick-and-dirty.
developer_profile.key_rule: Never suggest minimal or simplified designs. Always research the most advanced, sophisticated approach.
developer_profile.commit_rule: Never include Co-Authored-By lines referencing Claude or Anthropic. Marcus pushes manually.

agent_sequence.owns: graphify-out/graph-compare.html — HTML skeleton, CSS flex layout, dark theme, draggable divider
agent_sequence.deliverable: graph-compare.html renders two empty labeled panes with working drag divider. No graph data.

agent_sequence.owns: graph-compare.html — GraphRenderer object, vis-network CDN tag, fetch() calls, both pane renders
agent_sequence.deliverable: Both panes render graph data. Left = fixed (graph.json), Right = original (graph-original.json).

agent_sequence.owns: graph-compare.html — switcher button logic, inline markdown parser, GRAPH_REPORT.md fetch
agent_sequence.deliverable: Right pane switches between graph and GRAPH_REPORT without page reload.

agent_sequence.owns: graph-compare.html — annotation drawer markup, CSS, localStorage script
agent_sequence.deliverable: Drawer opens/closes, notes persist across reload, Copy All Notes outputs clean text.
Script runs, graph-original.json is valid JSON matching graph.json schema.
agent_sequence.dependency: None — can run in parallel with Agents 1-4

agent_sequence.owns: Read-only verification — walks all 8 acceptance criteria

acceptance_criteria: All three panels accessible without page reload
Annotations persist in localStorage across browser sessions
Copy All Notes produces clean plain text for pasting into Claude
Standalone HTML — opens from filesystem (file:// or python -m http.server)
GraphRenderer abstraction in place — vis-network never called from layout code
No external dependencies except vis-network CDN
graph-original.json extracted correctly from git history

locked_decisions.no_iframes: Single rendering host — graph-compare.html owns both graph renders directly
locked_decisions.renderer_interface: GraphRenderer.init/destroy/fit/on — vis-network never called from layout code
locked_decisions.json_first: fetch() at runtime — no embedded RAW_NODES/RAW_EDGES globals
locked_decisions.no_cdn_markdown: Inline parser only — no marked.js, no CDN for markdown
locked_decisions.vis_cdn_ok: vis-network from unpkg CDN is acceptable — compare tool is a dev tool
locked_decisions.config_object: All tuneable values in CONFIG object at top of file — never hardcoded below
